headline: Schonfeld Strategic Advisors Trims ETHA Stake as Ethereum ETFs Expand Regulated Access to Ether
summary: Schonfeld Strategic Advisors reduced its position in the iShares Ethereum Trust ETF during the fourth quarter. The fund holds ether directly and offers investors a simpler path to Ethereum exposure through a regulated exchange-traded structure, without the need to use crypto exchanges or manage digital wallets.

headline: Oracle sees AI boom through at least 2027, sending shares up 8%
summary:
STORY: :: Oracle

Oracle on Tuesday predicted that the AI data center boom will power its revenue above Wall Street estimates well into 2027.

That sent shares in the firm up over 8% in extended trading.

Oracle reported third quarter revenue of just over $17 billion, beating estimates.

It expects fourth-quarter revenue growth of up to 21%.

Tuesday's results helped allay investor concerns over Oracle's costly multi-billion dollar push into AI computing.

:: Oracle

:: File

The company has made a dramatic turn toward building data centers for partners such as OpenAI and Meta.

At the same time, it enacted layoffs as it uses smaller engineering teams and AI coding tools to roll out new software.

:: Oracle

:: File

Oracle has also been aggressively spending to expand its cloud infrastructure to support generative AI, competing against hyperscalers such as Amazon's AWS and Microsoft's Azure.

On a conference call with investors, CEO Clay Magouyrk said that the company's margins on its cloud business should improve over time.

headline: Microsoft aims to be multi-model: Exec talks Anthropic AI team-up
summary: In a new AI team-up, Microsoft (MSFT) is partnering with Anthropic (ANTH.PVT) to integrate its Claude Cowork chatbot into Microsoft's 365 suite and Copilot program. Microsoft president of business apps and agents Charles Lamanna sits down with Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley to discuss on the capabilities and tasks that Copilot Cowork can take on, while also commenting on the future for Microsoft's AI model development. headline: Wall Street Is Optimistic That Worst of Software Wipeout Is Over
summary: The S&P 500 software index is coming off its best week since May. The widely followed iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF, ticker IGV, just posted its strongest week in 11 months and is up 14% since Feb. 23, when Citrini Research rattled the market with its dystopian vision of an AI future.  A Goldman Sachs software basket is trading for 22 times forward earnings, compared with 21 for the S&P 500 Index.  Over the past decade, the basket has traded at an average multiple of 52, while the S&P 500’s average is 19.

content.title: Wall Street Is Optimistic That Worst of Software Wipeout Is Over
content.summary: The S&P 500 software index is coming off its best week since May. The widely followed iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF, ticker IGV, just posted its strongest week in 11 months and is up 14% since Feb. 23, when Citrini Research rattled the market with its dystopian vision of an AI future.  A Goldman Sachs software basket is trading for 22 times forward earnings, compared with 21 for the S&P 500 Index.  Over the past decade, the basket has traded at an average multiple of 52, while the S&P 500’s average is 19.

headline: Anthropic Tells Judge Billions at Stake If US Shuns AI Tool
summary: The startup made a case for urgency to US District Judge Rita F. Lin at a hearing in San Francisco a day after Anthropic sued the Defense Department over the supply-risk designation.  Microsoft Corp., which owns a stake in OpenAI and Anthropic, meanwhile filed its own brief urging a judge to temporarily block the government’s moves because they have the potential to delay all ongoing Defense Department “contracting for IT products and services.”  Michael Mongan, an attorney for Anthropic, argued Tuesday that the federal government’s actions have led to more than 100 enterprise customers contacting the company to express doubt about continuing their work with Anthropic.

content.title: Anthropic Tells Judge Billions at Stake If US Shuns AI Tool
content.summary: The startup made a case for urgency to US District Judge Rita F. Lin at a hearing in San Francisco a day after Anthropic sued the Defense Department over the supply-risk designation.  Microsoft Corp., which owns a stake in OpenAI and Anthropic, meanwhile filed its own brief urging a judge to temporarily block the government’s moves because they have the potential to delay all ongoing Defense Department “contracting for IT products and services.”  Michael Mongan, an attorney for Anthropic, argued Tuesday that the federal government’s actions have led to more than 100 enterprise customers contacting the company to express doubt about continuing their work with Anthropic.

headline: Musk unveils joint Tesla-xAI project 'Macrohard', eyes software disruption
summary: Elon Musk on Wednesday unveiled a joint project between Tesla and his artificial intelligence startup xAI, which ‌he called "Macrohard" or "Digital Optimus" and said that it was a ‌system capable of emulating the functions of software companies.  In a post on his social ​media platform X, Musk said the project pairs xAI's Grok large language model, acting as a high-level "navigator," with a Tesla-developed AI agent that processes real-time computer screen video and keyboard and mouse actions.  The launch of ‌Anthropic's Claude Cowork, which ⁠can perform a range of computer-based tasks autonomously, has already spooked software investors who fear that agentic AI ⁠can disrupt established business models.

content.title: Musk unveils joint Tesla-xAI project 'Macrohard', eyes software disruption
content.summary: Elon Musk on Wednesday unveiled a joint project between Tesla and his artificial intelligence startup xAI, which ‌he called "Macrohard" or "Digital Optimus" and said that it was a ‌system capable of emulating the functions of software companies.  In a post on his social ​media platform X, Musk said the project pairs xAI's Grok large language model, acting as a high-level "navigator," with a Tesla-developed AI agent that processes real-time computer screen video and keyboard and mouse actions.  The launch of ‌Anthropic's Claude Cowork, which ⁠can perform a range of computer-based tasks autonomously, has already spooked software investors who fear that agentic AI ⁠can disrupt established business models.

headline: U.S. Medical Company Hit With Suspected Iran-Linked Cyberattack
summary: Medical technology company Stryker is experiencing a global outage across its systems, with staff and contractors reporting that the logo of an Iran-linked hacking group has appeared on login pages.  Staff found that remote devices running Microsoft’s Windows operating system—such as cellphones, laptops and others configured to connect to Stryker’s technology systems—had been wiped.  A successful attack on a major U.S. company would be a significant escalation in a cyber conflict that had been expected to follow the U.S. and Israel’s military campaign in Iran, which began on Feb. 28.

content.title: U.S. Medical Company Hit With Suspected Iran-Linked Cyberattack
content.summary: Medical technology company Stryker is experiencing a global outage across its systems, with staff and contractors reporting that the logo of an Iran-linked hacking group has appeared on login pages.  Staff found that remote devices running Microsoft’s Windows operating system—such as cellphones, laptops and others configured to connect to Stryker’s technology systems—had been wiped.  A successful attack on a major U.S. company would be a significant escalation in a cyber conflict that had been expected to follow the U.S. and Israel’s military campaign in Iran, which began on Feb. 28.

headline: Microsoft backs Anthropic in legal fight over Pentagon AI ban
summary: Microsoft Corp (NASDAQ:MSFT) has filed a legal brief supporting Anthropic in its challenge to the Pentagon’s designation of the AI company as a “supply-chain risk,” which effectively barred it from federal contracts. The Department of Defense ended all contracts with Anthropic last month...

headline: Oracle Gets Customers To Start Chipping In—Heard on the Street
summary: Worries about Oracle’s ability to pay for all the AI deals it has struck cost the company more than half its market value over the past six months.  Getting customers to shoulder some of the load is repairing some of that damage.  Revenue and earnings for the February-ending period beat Wall Street’s estimates, and the company even managed to grow its substantial book of expected future revenue: Remaining performance obligations rose by $30 billion to hit $553 billion.

content.title: Oracle Gets Customers To Start Chipping In—Heard on the Street
content.summary: Worries about Oracle’s ability to pay for all the AI deals it has struck cost the company more than half its market value over the past six months.  Getting customers to shoulder some of the load is repairing some of that damage.  Revenue and earnings for the February-ending period beat Wall Street’s estimates, and the company even managed to grow its substantial book of expected future revenue: Remaining performance obligations rose by $30 billion to hit $553 billion.

headline: Hims & Hers C-suite shake-up, Stryker outage may be Iran-backed
summary: Yahoo Finance anchor Josh Lipton tracks Wednesday's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute. Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) stock is soaring after the company named Katherine Beir as its next chief communications officer, reporting to CEO Andrew Dudum. Stryker Corporation (SYK) stock is under pressure amid a global outage that could be tied to an Iran-backed hacking group. headline: US medical equipment company Stryker says cyberattack disrupted its global networks.
summary: Stryker, a major U.S. medical equipment company, said a cyberattack disrupted its global networks Wednesday.  Stryker's statement said the cyberattack hit its Microsoft programs.  Stryker is based in Portage, Michigan, and makes a variety of medical products, from artificial joints to hospital beds.

Stryker is based in Portage, Michigan, and makes a variety of medical products, from artificial joints to hospital beds.

headline: Meta to develop 4 new AI chips. Can it stack up to the competition?
summary: Meta Platforms (META) has unveiled that it is developing four new chips — the MTIA 300, 400, 450, and 500 semiconductors — to power its AI and recommendation systems across platforms. Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley comes on Market Domination to break down the day’s leading and trending tech stories, including pondering how Meta's new AI chips may stack up to other chip giants.
